title: In NATO’s Next Act, Can Europe Lead?
summary: The focus at a leaders’ summit in Turkey this week will be on building a new model for the alliance, as President Trump pushes to do less and pressures others do more.

title: Trump Expected to Tell Turkey He Is Ready to Restore Access to F-35 Jets
summary: The president, who is headed to a NATO summit in Ankara this week, had imposed the ban himself amid concerns that giving Turkey the jets could allow Russia to learn about their stealth technology.

title: What to Watch at the NATO Summit, and Platner’s Allies Rescind Endorsements
summary: Plus, the museum that wants you to touch the art.

title: Court Upholds Marine Le Pen’s Conviction, but Leaves Path to Presidency
summary: Though it confirmed Ms. Le Pen’s embezzlement conviction, the court also shortened a ban on her eligibility for elected office. That could allow the far-right leader to run for the presidency next year.

title: Abandoned by Allies, Graham Platner Faces Pressure to End Maine Senate Campaign
summary: After Graham Platner, the Democratic nominee in Maine, was accused of rape, much of the party and several key supporters turned against him.

title: In CNN Interview, Woman Details Alleged Sexual Assault by Graham Platner
summary: “This is something that I tried for many years to forget,” the woman, Jenny Racicot, said on CNN.

title: Who Might Replace Graham Platner if He Drops Out of the Maine Senate Race?
summary: Graham Platner can be replaced on the ballot if he withdraws in the next week. If he does, Maine Democrats would face an uncertain two-week race to choose a replacement.

title: Trump Promised a Foreign Investment Boom. It’s Getting Harder to Deliver.
summary: The threat of tariffs may have helped push more capital into the United States, but other factors are pushing it away.

title: U.S. Trade Deficit Widens in May on Record Goods Imports
summary: Imports of foreign goods, including pharmaceuticals and equipment for data centers, hit a record high, pushing the monthly trade deficit to its highest level in over a year.

title: New York’s Best Burgers
summary: The best of New York’s dynamic dining scene (mostly) between two buns.

title: Inside Trump’s Ideological Fight With the Smithsonian
summary: Publicly and behind the scenes, the president continues to try to impose his own views of American history and culture, presenting an ongoing challenge to Lonnie Bunch, the institution’s leader.

title: Anthea Hartig, Smithsonian Museum Director, Faces the White House’s Wrath
summary: Historians defended Anthea Hartig, director of the National Museum of American History, after the Trump administration accused her of spreading “radical activist ideology.”

title: The History at the Smithsonian That the White House Finds Unpatriotic
summary: The administration’s report criticizing the National Museum of American History echoes themes of President Trump’s push to reshape the American story.

title: McConnell Has Been Hospitalized for 3 Weeks, and Aides Won’t Say Why
summary: Senator Mitch McConnell was admitted to the hospital on June 14, and his office has provided few updates about his condition. Here’s what we know.

title: How a Four-Generation Cuban Family Survives on $60 a Month
summary: The U.S. oil blockade has deepened a humanitarian crisis, forcing Cubans to lean on the island’s long tradition of community solidarity to provide a cushion.

title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s Slain Leader, Is Mourned in Qom
summary: The ceremony for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, held at a site considered sacred by many Shiites, was the latest step in a weeklong funeral organized by Iran’s government.

title: What Our Reporter Saw in Tehran: A Human Tide of Mourners
summary: After days of public mourning ceremonies in the Iranian capital, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s coffin arrived in Tehran’s Azadi Square. This is what I saw covering the miles-long procession on Monday.

title: Iran’s Strikes on Ships in Strait of Hormuz Test Cease-Fire and Threaten Oil Flow
summary: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has picked up since Iran and the U.S. agreed to try to negotiate a lasting end to their hostilities, but risks remain.

title: Estonia Won the War on Fentanyl. What Came Next Was Even Worse.
summary: By 2018, fentanyl overdoses in Estonia had plummeted. But powerful new drugs are appearing fast, with the authorities racing to respond.

title: Inside the United States’ Billion-Dollar Blind Spot: Hail
summary: Scientists have never quite understood how hail works. Meet the storm chasers trying to change that.

title: Death Toll From Oppressive Heat in New Jersey May Be as High as 29
summary: In New York City, emergency rooms last week logged the highest number of patients treated for heat-related illnesses in years.

title: The New York City Plumber Who Made Over $465,000 in a Year
summary: A public housing employee made more than $465,000 in a year with overtime, while apparently running two private plumbing businesses at the same time. The city is investigating.

title: Lessons From the Graham Platner Disaster
summary: Many Democrats want representatives as furious as they are. That makes it easier for unfit candidates to escape proper vetting.

title: JD Vance Is Worried
summary: The vice president’s theology comes in book form.

title: Caribbean Disparity
summary: We look at U.S. relief efforts in Haiti in 2010 and in Venezuela now.

title: ‘Kimmel’ Guest Host Tiffany Haddish Roasts Trump’s Fourth of July Party
summary: The comedian and actress joked that “there was more people at my bat mitzvah” than at America’s 250th birthday party.

title: Why China Fired a Long-Range Missile Into the Pacific
summary: Beijing had long tested nuclear missiles only within its borders. A rare launch over an ocean shows it is determined to narrow the gap with the United States.
